name: dockerfile-parse

metadata.name: dockerfile-parse
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
# dockerfile-parse

[![unittests status badge]][unittests status link]
[![coveralls status badge]][coveralls status link]
[![lgtm python badge]][lgtm python link]
[![lgtm alerts badge]][lgtm alerts link]
[![linters status badge]][linters status link]

Python library for parsing Dockerfile files.

## Installation

### From PyPI

```shell
pip install dockerfile-parse
```

### From git

Clone this git repo and install using the python installer

```shell
git clone https://github.com/containerbuildsystem/dockerfile-parse.git
cd dockerfile-parse
sudo pip install .
```

## Usage

```python
from pprint import pprint
from dockerfile_parse import DockerfileParser

dfp = DockerfileParser()
dfp.content = """\
From  base
LABEL foo="bar baz"
USER  me"""

# Print the parsed structure:
pprint(dfp.structure)
pprint(dfp.json)
pprint(dfp.labels)

# Set a new base:
dfp.baseimage = 'centos:7'

# Print the new Dockerfile with an updated FROM line:
print(dfp.content)
```

methods.name: dockerfile_parse.util.WordSplitter._update_quoting_state
documentation:
Update self.quotes and self.escaped

```
param ch

str, current character

return

ch if it was not used to update quoting state, else ''
```

methods.name: dockerfile_parse.util.WordSplitter.split
documentation:
Generator for the words of the string

```
param maxsplit

perform at most maxsplit splits;
if None, do not limit the number of splits

param dequote

remove quotes and escape characters once consumed
```

Split string into words, substituting environment variables if provided

Methods defined here:

dequote()
    Returns the string with escaped and quotes consumed

split(maxsplit=None, dequote=True)
    Returns an iterable of words, split at whitespace

methods.name: dockerfile_parse.util.Context.__init__
documentation:
Class representing current state of build arguments, environment variables and labels.

```
param args

dict with arguments valid for this line
(all variables defined to this line)

param envs

dict with variables valid for this line
(all variables defined to this line)

param labels

dict with labels valid for this line
(all labels defined to this line)

param line_args

dict with arguments defined on this line

param line_envs

dict with variables defined on this line

param line_labels

dict with labels defined on this line
```

methods.name: dockerfile_parse.util.Context.set_line_value
documentation:
Set value defined on this line ('line_args'/'line_envs'/'line_labels')
and update 'args'/'envs'/'labels'.

```
param context_type

"ARG" or "ENV" or "LABEL"

param value

new value for this line
```

methods.name: dockerfile_parse.util.Context.get_line_value
documentation:
Get the values defined on this line.

```
param context_type

"ARG" or "ENV" or "LABEL"

return

values of given type defined on this line
```

methods.name: dockerfile_parse.util.Context.get_values
documentation:
Get the values valid on this line.

```
param context_type

"ARG" or "ENV" or "LABEL"

return

values of given type valid on this line
```

functions.name: dockerfile_parse.parser.image_from
documentation:
---
param from_value: string like "image:tag" or "image:tag AS name"
return: tuple of the image and stage name, e.g. ("image:tag", None)
---

functions.name: dockerfile_parse.parser._endline
documentation:
Make sure the line ends with a single newline.
Since trailing whitespace has no significance, remove it.

classes.name: dockerfile_parse.parser.KeyValues
documentation:
Abstract base class for allowing direct write access to Dockerfile
instructions which result in a set of key value pairs.

Subclasses must override the `parser_attr` value.

classes.name: dockerfile_parse.parser.Labels
documentation:
A class for allowing direct write access to Dockerfile labels, e.g.:

parser.labels['label'] = 'value'

classes.name: dockerfile_parse.parser.Envs
documentation:
A class for allowing direct write access to Dockerfile env. vars., e.g.:

parser.envs['variable_name'] = 'value'

classes.name: dockerfile_parse.parser.Args
documentation:
A class for allowing direct write access to Dockerfile build args, e.g.:

parser.args['variable_name'] = 'value'

methods.name: dockerfile_parse.parser.DockerfileParser.__init__
documentation:
Initialize source of Dockerfile
\:param path: path to (directory with) Dockerfile; if not provided,

> and fileobj is not provided, the current working
> directory will be used

```
param cache_content

cache Dockerfile content inside DockerfileParser

param env_replace

return content with variables replaced

param parent_env

python dict of inherited env vars from parent image

param fileobj

seekable file-like object containing Dockerfile content
as bytes (will be truncated on write)

param build_args

python dict of build args used when building image
```

methods.name: dockerfile_parse.parser.DockerfileParser.structure
documentation:
Returns a list of dicts describing the commands:
        [
            {"instruction": "FROM",       # always upper-case
             "startline": 0,              # 0-based
             "endline": 0,                # 0-based
             "content": "From fedora
",
             "value": "fedora"},

            {"instruction": "CMD",
             "startline": 1,
             "endline": 2,
             "content": "CMD yum -y update && \
    yum clean all
",
             "value": "yum -y update && yum clean all"}
        ]

methods.name: dockerfile_parse.parser.DockerfileParser.json
documentation:
---
return: JSON formatted string with instructions & values from Dockerfile
---

methods.name: dockerfile_parse.parser.DockerfileParser.parent_images
documentation:
---
return: list of parent images -- one image per each stage's FROM instruction
---

methods.name: dockerfile_parse.parser.DockerfileParser.parent_images
documentation:
setter for images in 'FROM' instructions.
Images are updated per build stage with the given parents in the order they appear.
Raises RuntimeError if a different number of parents are given than there are stages
as that is likely to be a mistake.

```
param parents

list of image strings
```

methods.name: dockerfile_parse.parser.DockerfileParser.baseimage
documentation:
---
return: base image, i.e. value of final stage FROM instruction
---

documentation: change image of final stage FROM instruction

methods.name: dockerfile_parse.parser.DockerfileParser.cmd
documentation:
Determine the final CMD instruction, if any, in the final build stage.
CMDs from earlier stages are ignored.
\:return: value of final stage CMD instruction

methods.name: dockerfile_parse.parser.DockerfileParser._delete_instructions
documentation:
---
param instruction: name of instruction to be deleted
param value: |-

---

documentation:
---
param instruction: instruction name to be added
param value: instruction value
---

methods.name: dockerfile_parse.parser.DockerfileParser.add_lines
documentation:
Add lines to the beginning or end of the build.
\:param lines: one or more lines to add to the content, by default at the end.
\:param all_stages: bool for whether to add in all stages for a multistage build

> or (by default) only the last.

```
param at_start

adds at the beginning (after FROM) of the stage(s) instead of the end.

param skip_scratch

skip stages which use "FROM scratch"
```

methods.name: dockerfile_parse.parser.DockerfileParser.add_lines_at
documentation:
Add lines at a specific location in the file.
\:param anchor: structure_dict|line_str|line_num a reference to where adds should occur
\:param lines: one or more lines to add to the content
\:param replace: if True -- replace the anchor
\:param after: if True -- insert after the anchor (conflicts with "replace")
